comment: Partner-identification scan. The GREEN gate requires >=2 partners with active inhaled-mRNA programs forkable into IL-1Ra. CDMOs are listed at the OE discovery-stage outreach scale — companies that have published a respiratory-mRNA or inhaled-mRNA capability and would respond to a target-validation handoff package from a research project.

tier_A_inhaled_mrna_developers_with_clin.name: Arcturus Therapeutics
tier_A_inhaled_mrna_developers_with_clin.fork_potential: HIGH — full inhaled-mRNA platform stack (LUNAR LNP + STARR self-amplifying mRNA + nebulizer formulation). Could swap CFTR cassette for IL-1Ra cassette as a research collaboration.
tier_A_inhaled_mrna_developers_with_clin.size: Public, ~$200-500M market cap range; small-pharma scale
tier_A_inhaled_mrna_developers_with_clin.outreach_path: Academic / nonprofit collaboration program; BD team at Arcturus

tier_A_inhaled_mrna_developers_with_clin.name: ReCode Therapeutics
tier_A_inhaled_mrna_developers_with_clin.program: RCT2100 (inhaled mRNA-DNAI1 for PCD) + CF program
tier_A_inhaled_mrna_developers_with_clin.fork_potential: HIGH — SORT-LNP class adds lung tropism beyond passive deposition. Same swap (DNAI1 -> IL-1Ra) is straightforward.
tier_A_inhaled_mrna_developers_with_clin.size: Private; ~$200M Series B 2023; Pfizer collaboration on rare lung disease
tier_A_inhaled_mrna_developers_with_clin.outreach_path: BD team; rare-lung-disease network; Pfizer collaboration could be a back-channel

tier_A_inhaled_mrna_developers_with_clin.name: Ethris
tier_A_inhaled_mrna_developers_with_clin.fork_potential: MEDIUM — chemically modified mRNA platform with explicit inhalation focus; AstraZeneca partnership signals commercial path

tier_A_inhaled_mrna_developers_with_clin.name: Sanofi (legacy Translate Bio MRT5005)
tier_A_inhaled_mrna_developers_with_clin.program: MRT5005 wound down 2022; inhaled-mRNA platform IP retained
tier_A_inhaled_mrna_developers_with_clin.stage: Platform IP only (no active inhaled clinical program)
tier_A_inhaled_mrna_developers_with_clin.fork_potential: MEDIUM — Sanofi has the manufacturing+formulation know-how but no current clinical lung-mRNA program; would need to re-energize platform around a new target. IL-1Ra in gout is a novel indication that fits Sanofi's autoimmune/inflammation portfolio

tier_B_cdmo_with_inhaled_or_pulmonary_ca.name: CordenPharma
tier_B_cdmo_with_inhaled_or_pulmonary_ca.capability: mRNA-LNP CDMO at clinical scale; lipid manufacturing; multiple mRNA partners
tier_B_cdmo_with_inhaled_or_pulmonary_ca.fork_potential: MEDIUM — would manufacture the construct but not provide formulation/device IP; partner with a Tier A for the formulation stack

tier_B_cdmo_with_inhaled_or_pulmonary_ca.name: Aldevron (Danaher)
tier_B_cdmo_with_inhaled_or_pulmonary_ca.fork_potential: MEDIUM — same shape as CordenPharma; manufacturing partner, not formulation

tier_B_cdmo_with_inhaled_or_pulmonary_ca.name: Lonza
tier_B_cdmo_with_inhaled_or_pulmonary_ca.capability: mRNA-LNP CDMO; multiple Phase 1-3 client programs
tier_B_cdmo_with_inhaled_or_pulmonary_ca.fork_potential: MEDIUM — large CDMO with capacity; not inhalation-specific

tier_B_cdmo_with_inhaled_or_pulmonary_ca.name: Recipharm
tier_B_cdmo_with_inhaled_or_pulmonary_ca.capability: Inhalation device + drug-device combination CDMO (DPI + nebulizer expertise)
tier_B_cdmo_with_inhaled_or_pulmonary_ca.fork_potential: HIGH for the device-formulation side specifically — Recipharm makes DPI and aerosol-delivery devices. Would partner with a Tier A or mRNA CDMO for the mRNA-LNP itself.

tier_B_cdmo_with_inhaled_or_pulmonary_ca.name: Acuitas Therapeutics
tier_B_cdmo_with_inhaled_or_pulmonary_ca.capability: LNP IP holder (BioNTech BNT162b2 lipid); not a manufacturer but licenses LNP
tier_B_cdmo_with_inhaled_or_pulmonary_ca.fork_potential: MEDIUM — LNP license required for many programs; not inhaled-specific but their LP-01 class is being adapted for inhalation by partners

tier_B_cdmo_with_inhaled_or_pulmonary_ca.name: Precision NanoSystems (Cytiva)
tier_B_cdmo_with_inhaled_or_pulmonary_ca.capability: LNP manufacturing equipment + early-stage LNP formulation services

tier_B_cdmo_with_inhaled_or_pulmonary_ca.name: TriLink BioTechnologies (Maravai)
tier_B_cdmo_with_inhaled_or_pulmonary_ca.fork_potential: MEDIUM — supplier of the mRNA itself; not formulation or device

tier_C_academic_labs_with_inhaled_mrna_l.name: Mitchell Lab (UPenn)
tier_C_academic_labs_with_inhaled_mrna_l.publications: Multiple inhaled mRNA-LNP papers 2021-2025; SORT-LNP class for lung; co-author network with ReCode
tier_C_academic_labs_with_inhaled_mrna_l.fork_potential: HIGH — academic collaboration possible; publish-mode-of-engagement aligned with OE open-source ethos

tier_C_academic_labs_with_inhaled_mrna_l.name: Sahay Lab (OSU / Oregon State)
tier_C_academic_labs_with_inhaled_mrna_l.publications: LNP formulation for nucleic acid delivery; inhaled and systemic; multiple lung-mRNA papers

tier_C_academic_labs_with_inhaled_mrna_l.name: Anderson Lab (MIT)
tier_C_academic_labs_with_inhaled_mrna_l.publications: LNP combinatorial libraries; some lung-tropic work; broad LNP IP
tier_C_academic_labs_with_inhaled_mrna_l.fork_potential: MEDIUM — large lab, many active collaborations; smaller probability of OE-specific bandwidth

tier_C_academic_labs_with_inhaled_mrna_l.name: Siegwart Lab (UT Southwestern)
tier_C_academic_labs_with_inhaled_mrna_l.publications: SORT-LNP foundational work (origin of ReCode platform); lung-tropic LNPs
tier_C_academic_labs_with_inhaled_mrna_l.fork_potential: HIGH — direct origin of the lung-tropic LNP class

green_gate_check: GREEN requires >=2 partners with active inhaled-mRNA programs identified. Tier A alone has 4 candidates with disclosed clinical or preclinical inhaled-mRNA programs (Arcturus, ReCode, Ethris, Sanofi). Gate cleared by Tier A alone.
recommended_first_contacts: Siegwart Lab UTSW (academic origin of lung-tropic LNP class — could provide a precompetitive scientific collaboration)
Mitchell Lab UPenn (academic; multiple inhaled mRNA-LNP papers)
